summary: Matched plbonuslib function fn_8003F294 by replacing its placeholder comment with a C implementation in src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c. The PR body reports require-protos, targeted plbonuslib.o build, full ninja, and local objdiff at 680 bytes / 100.0%; the bot report independently records one new match for main/melee/pl/plbonuslib fn_8003F294, +680 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; adds inline wrapper plBonusLib_8003F294_inline and implementation of fn_8003F294.

what_changed.text: Replaced the placeholder comment `/// fn_8003F294` with a full C definition for `void fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: -/// fn_8003F294 / +void fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)

what_changed.text: Added `inline unsigned int plBonusLib_8003F294_inline(plActionStats* stats, int attack)` as a narrow wrapper around `pl_800386D8(stats, attack)`.

The new function works through `Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2`, `Player_GetActionStats`, repeated `pl_8003906C` calls, one `pl_80039238` magnitude update using `sqrtf__Ff`, and bitfield updates on `table->xDD1.bit5`.

The PR reports verification with `python configure.py --require-protos`, targeted `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.o`, full `ninja`, and local objdiff showing `fn_8003F294`: 680 bytes, 100.0%.
The automated report records one new match in `main/melee/pl/plbonuslib`: `fn_8003F294`, +680 bytes, 0.00% before to 100.00% after.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev[bot] Report for GALE01, 1 new match

smart_moves.text: Kept the match focused to one translation unit and one function, with only one changed file and one hunk.
Included both prototype checking and object/full builds in the PR verification notes, which is useful when adding a new function body and inline helper.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: python configure.py --require-protos; ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.o; ninja

smart_moves.text: Used existing typed structures and fields such as `plActionStats`, `pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t`, `stats->hits.total`, `stats->attacks.total`, `table->x0_staleMoveTable.xCDC`, and `table->xDD1.bit5` rather than representing all accesses as raw byte offsets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8003F294 declarations and field accesses

decomp_lessons.text: For a newly matched function body, pair the local objdiff claim with an automated progress report when available; here both the PR body and bot report support the same +680 byte, 100% match for `fn_8003F294`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `fn_8003F294`: 680 bytes, 100.0% in the local objdiff report

decomp_lessons.text: When implementing player bonus/stat code, typed project structures can express many recovered accesses directly even while some fields still retain offset-style names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: plActionStats* stats; pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t* table; stats->hits.total; stats->attacks.total; table->xDD1.bit5

naming_conventions.text: The matched function kept the existing address-style symbol name `fn_8003F294`, consistent with conservative naming when the PR evidence does not prove a semantic function name.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void fn_8003F294(int slot, int index)

naming_conventions.text: The added local inline wrapper was named with subsystem/function context, `plBonusLib_8003F294_inline`, and only wraps `pl_800386D8` for this function's call shape.
Several recovered locals retain match-oriented names such as `new_var`, `threshold`, `threshold2`, `t2`, `x8c`, and `xb8`; the PR evidence does not explain semantic meanings for these names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local declarations and inner block locals

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used `PAD_STACK(24)` inside `fn_8003F294`, implying a stack-shape matching tactic. The PR body reports 100% local objdiff for the function but does not separately justify this tactic.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `fn_8003F294`: 680 bytes, 100.0% in the local objdiff report

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Introduced a tiny inline wrapper around `pl_800386D8`; this may have been used to recover a desired inline/call code shape, but the PR text does not state the reason.

Used assignment expressions inside function-call arguments, e.g. `threshold = pl_804D6470->x94` and `new_var = pl_804D6470->xBC`, which appears to preserve a specific evaluation/local lifetime shape for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated pl_8003906C calls with assignment expressions

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a `do { if (index == 1) return; } while (0);` shape instead of a plain early-return `if`; this is likely match-driven, but no review comment or PR body note explains it.
Re-fetched the stale move table into `t2` for the magnitude calculation instead of reusing the earlier `table` pointer, preserving a separate access shape in that block.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.
Automated bot feedback reported one new match and +680 matched bytes for `fn_8003F294`.

follow_up_queries: do while 0 early return index == 1 plbonuslib

notes: Evidence is strong for PR identity, changed file, function added, and reported match result.
There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.

Several code shapes look match-driven, including PAD_STACK, do-while(0), assignment expressions in arguments, and a narrow inline wrapper, but the PR does not explicitly explain why those shapes were required.
The intake did not independently rerun builds or objdiff; verification is recorded as PR/body and bot-report evidence only.

source_update_candidates.title: Record PAD_STACK justification alongside local objdiff evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR used `PAD_STACK(24)` in a newly matched function and reported 100% local objdiff, but did not separately document why the stack padding was necessary. Curator may consider whether standards should ask authors to call out PAD_STACK-specific justification in PR bodies when it appears in new source.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already say matching tactics need evidence; this PR is a concrete example where the final match evidence exists but tactic-specific reasoning is not described.

source_update_candidates.title: plbonuslib contains player bonus/stat stale move table matching work
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2565 shows `src/melee/pl/plbonuslib.c` implementing `fn_8003F294` using player action stats, stale move table fields, bonus threshold globals, and plbonuslib helper calls.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path-scope context for future search; should be curator-reviewed before becoming a source-owned path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer semantic meanings for `fn_8003F294`, `new_var`, `threshold`, `threshold2`, or offset-style fields solely from this PR slice.
Do not promote the inline wrapper, `do { ... } while (0)`, or assignment-in-argument patterns as general style; they are only observed match tactics here.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details beyond merge state; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
